title: FLA Return Filing Online in India – Fees, Cost & Consultant
metaDescription: What is FLA return and how to file FLA return? Get FLA return filing online with documents required, fees, cost India & expert consultant service support.

hero.headline: FLA Return Filing in India – Online Process, Fees & Documents
hero.subtext: Trusted by Indian companies for RBI-compliant FLA submissions. On-time, accurate & fully managed.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The FLA (Foreign Liabilities and Assets) Return is required to be filed by all the companies who have received FDI or made ODI in an Indian entity in the latest financial year(s) such reporting is required to be submitted directly by all the Indian companies which have received FDI or made ODI in the IETP (Indian Entity Type) in that year. This is filed under FEMA 1999 and is a requirement of the RBI. the FLA should be submitted on or before July 15th annually on FLAIR (FLA Online Reporting) Portal. The return provides crucial information about foreign assets, liabilities and performance. Professionals Law is also engaged in assisting with drafting, validation and filling of Return Filling (FLA) for complete compliance with law and in order not to invite FEMA Penalties

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: FLA return is required to ensure transparency in India’s foreign exchange transactions. It enables RBI to access the information on cross-border investments and external financial exposure of Indian companies. A failure to file or the inclusion of inaccurate information can trigger regulatory inspection and sanctions. FLA filing is also required for fund raising, due diligence and overseas transactions. Professionals Law helps you file your FLA return correctly by checking your financial data, following RBI instructions to fit the format, and uploading the information onto the RBI FLAIR Portal before the deadline.

body.title: Ensures FEMA Compliance
body.description: Filing the FLA return ensures compliance with the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) and prevents legal or financial penalties from the Reserve Bank of India.

body.title: Boosts Business Credibility
body.description: Accurate reporting of foreign investments helps improve transparency, which enhances credibility with foreign investors, lenders and regulatory bodies.

body.title: Essential for Fundraising
body.description: Timely FLA compliance is often required during funding rounds, investor audits or mergers and acquisitions involving international stakeholders.

body.title: Avoids Regulatory Penalties
body.description: Professionals Law ensures that your filings are completed well before the deadline, eliminating the risk of non-compliance or financial penalties.

body.title: Professional Representation
body.description: Professionals Law manages FLA submissions professionally, keeping your data secure while representing your interests before regulatory authorities.

categories.applicantType: Entities required to file FLA Return include:
criteria: Indian companies that have received FDI during the previous financial year or current year (even if shareholding was transferred later).
LLPs and other entities that have received FDI or made ODI.
Companies with outbound investments in foreign subsidiaries, associates or joint ventures.
Entities that reported FDI/ODI in previous years must file even if there is no change during the current year.

documents: Audited or unaudited financial statements of the entity
PAN and CIN of the company
Details of foreign equity investments received
Details of overseas direct investments made
Previous year’s FLA return data (if applicable)

body.description: Collect detailed information on inbound FDI and outbound ODI for the reporting year.
Entities must register with RBI’s FLAIR portal (https://flair.rbi.org.in).
Professionals Law prepares the return using the company’s financial and investment data, ensuring adherence to RBI’s prescribed format.
The completed FLA return is uploaded and submitted online through the FLAIR portal.
Professionals Law tracks submission status and provides confirmation of successful filing.

body.title: Non-filing or incorrect filing of the FLA Return may result in:

body.description: Penalty up to ₹2 lakh or thrice the sum involved, whichever is higher
Additional ₹5,000 per day for continuing contraventions
Reputational damage and regulatory scrutiny during due diligence
Professionals Law ensures timely filing and error-free compliance to eliminate these risks.
Due date for filing FLA return is 15th July, every year

body.question: Who must file FLA Return?
body.answer: All Indian entities receiving FDI or making ODI during the year must file.

body.question: What if no change occurred in FDI or ODI during the year?
body.answer: Filing is still mandatory if the entity reported such investments in previous years.

body.question: Is audited financial data mandatory?
body.answer: No, unaudited figures can be submitted if the audited financials are not ready by July 15.

body.question: What happens if I miss the filing deadline?
body.answer: Late filing may attract FEMA penalties and can impact business credibility.

body.question: Can Professionals Law assist with registration on the FLAIR portal?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law handles FLAIR portal registration and end-to-end return filing.

body.question: Can LLPs file the FLA Return?
body.answer: Yes, LLPs that have received FDI or made ODI are also required to file.

body.question: Is the FLA Return a one-time requirement?
body.answer: No, it must be filed every year if FDI or ODI exists or existed previously.

body.question: Can I revise the return once submitted?
body.answer: Yes, revised submissions are allowed before RBI accepts the return.

body.question: Does FLA filing apply to transfer of shares?
body.answer: Yes, if foreign shareholding exists due to transfer, it must be reported.
